name: Lin
observedShift: Lin has delivered two major features ahead of schedule but has worked late for about three weeks. I need to understand whether this pace was a temporary push or is becoming a default. Because Lin tends to brush off concern, I should ask directly what is driving the load now that those launches are done. I also want clarity on what work only Lin should own versus what could be delegated, since sustained late nights at this level can hide a distribution or autonomy problem.

focusPoints.label: Late nights — temporary push or default pace?
focusPoints.reason: Notes mention working late for about three weeks after delivering two major features ahead of schedule. Could be a short-term sprint, or the team may be relying on Lin to carry the load—worth clarifying which is true now that the launches are done.

focusPoints.label: What Lin is driving now that launches are done
focusPoints.reason: Whether the next cycle’s top work is truly the highest-leverage use of Lin’s time, or whether priorities are drifting into ongoing firefighting once the initial wins are over.

focusPoints.label: What Lin should delegate vs keep personally
focusPoints.reason: Whether sustained late nights are hiding a distribution or autonomy gap—worth getting explicit on which parts only Lin should own and which could move to others without losing quality or speed.

focusPoints.label: What he’d want more of from you
focusPoints.reason: How they want you to provide air cover and unblock decisions once the big launches are behind them—especially if they tend to brush off concern and need a clearer way to ask for help.
